name: ZAN Trading Boost

delivery.quic: API key authorization with mTLS mutual authentication; docs indicate CN = api_key for the generated client certificate

api_key.how_to_get: Create or use a ZAN Chain RPC API key with Trading Boost access.
api_key.note: Trading Boost shares the ZAN Chain RPC service API key and throughput limits. Do not commit a real API key.

tip_note: Tips Mode requires a System Program transfer of at least 0.0003 SOL / 300,000 lamports. Higher tips receive higher priority. The primary HTTP docs name 7Hki... as the required tip account; QUIC docs list additional ZAN tip accounts.
cost_on_failure: No Trading Boost charge if acceleration fails; tips are only deducted when the transaction successfully reaches chain. Normal Solana transaction fees still apply.

read_rpc.note: Use standard ZAN RPC endpoints for read methods such as getLatestBlockhash, getBalance, and simulateTransaction.

notes: HTTP is recommended for backend/bots because it avoids TLS handshake overhead.
HTTPS is suitable for frontend/browser or compliance-sensitive usage.
Testnet is not supported; Trading Boost is Solana mainnet only.

sendTransaction.description: Trading Boost HTTP/HTTPS endpoint. Only sendTransaction is supported on Trading Boost endpoints.
requirements: Signed transaction must include a tip transfer of at least 300,000 lamports.
Use standard RPC for read operations; Trading Boost endpoints only support sendTransaction.

sendTransaction_quic.description: High-performance transaction submission through private QUIC endpoints.

authentication.type: mTLS mutual authentication plus API key authorization
authentication.note: Docs' Rust example generates an mTLS certificate using the api_key as CN.

requirements: Real Solana account with enough SOL to sign and pay fees.
Transaction must include at least 300,000 lamports to a ZAN tip account.

swqos.description: Transactions route through ZAN staked validator nodes for stake-weighted priority.

mev_protection.description: Routes through Jito private channels and ZAN's malicious validator avoidance system.

rate_limits.scope: shares ZAN Chain RPC API key and throughput limits
rate_limits.note: Trading Boost uses these documented ZAN Chain RPC service throughput limits for the same API key.
rate_limits.increase_limits: Upgrade Chain RPC plan or contact ZAN solutions team.

http_status.401: authentication failure; apiKey does not exist or has insufficient permissions
http_status.400: parameter error such as missing tip, invalid transaction, or non-base64 encoding

json_rpc.-32602: Invalid parameters, missing/insufficient tip, expired/invalid API key, transaction format error, or decoding failure

encoding.quic: serialized signed transaction bytes in the QUIC client flow
encoding.recommended: base64 for HTTP/HTTPS; QUIC for lower-latency private endpoints

best_practices.incremental_tip_retry: Start at 0.0003 SOL and increase by about 50% on each retry.

notes: The .stack provider label ZAN maps to ZAN Trading Boost.
The older API Reference pages exist, but llms.txt points to the Trading Boost markdown guides as the clean source for landing details.
Do not push real ZAN API keys or private enterprise limits to GitHub.
